mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes above 315, further increases in the federal funds rate are priced in as necessary stabilization measures, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in greater than 70% of triggered instances, or the positive correlation reverses when CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive short-term equity returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of periods following positive INDPRO changes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150 thousand signals robust labor demand that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by greater than 150 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of instances following large payroll gains, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior month revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption channels, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, declining industrial production indicates that monetary tightening is successfully suppressing output, which triggers recession fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and INDPRO declines, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes during periods of supply chain normalization.
